revision.text:
[[ja:海士潜女神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ama Kazukime Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Ama Kazukime Shrine 01.jpg | location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Toba, Mie|en|Toba, Mie|qid=Q819629|lt=Toba City|1=Toba, Mie}}, {{ill|Kuzaki Town|ja|国崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuzaki Town|1=Kuzakichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11146786}} 312 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude minutes = 24 | Latitude seconds = 53 | Longitude = 136 | Longitude minutes = 55 | Longitude seconds = 27.9 | Label Position = left | Map = Japan Mie | Main deity = Kazukime-no-Kami and others | Shrine Rank = Village shrine | Establishment = Unknown | Honden = {{ill|Wooden structure|ja|木構造 (建築)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden structure|1=wooden structure|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1363743}} in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | Alternate name = Ama Gozen | festival = {{ill|June 1 (Lunisolar)|ja|6月1日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 1 (Lunisolar)|qq=}} → {{ill|July 1|en|July 1|qid=Q2700|lt=July 1|1=July 1}} | Rituals = {{ill|Hachiman Festival|ja|八幡祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Two Boat Festival|ja|二船祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Two Boat Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 海士潜女神社|1=Ama Kazukime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17191773}} | location = {{ill|Kuzakichō|qid=Q11146786|1=Kuzakichō}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.414722|N|136.924417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ama Kazukime Shrine''' (海士潜女神社, Ama Kazukime Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kuzaki Town|ja|国崎町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuzaki Town|1=Kuzakichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11146786}}, {{ill|Toba, Mie|en|Toba, Mie|qid=Q819629|lt=Toba City|1=Toba, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It was formerly known as Ama Gozen (海士御前) until 1869. While not under the administration of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}, it maintains a deep connection to Ise and enshrines the legendary {{ill|ama (female diver)|ja|海女|lt=Ama diver|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ama (female diver)|qq=}} Oben, attracting faith from divers across Japan. Ama Kazukime Shrine enshrines the legendary ama named Oben, who dedicated an abalone to Emperor's Princess, Yamatohime-no-mikoto. The shrine is regarded as having divine favor in preventing dizziness during diving and is worshiped by local amas and divers nation-wide.https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=5de8780f388380bac94109889b3b4851fbe31c06bec1a4e2bc67168448801156JmltdHM9MTc0MzYzODQwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=0baff627-1adb-6b49-298a-e3e31b796a0d&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9xcnRyYW5zbGF0b3IuY29tLzAwMDAwMDE3NDcvMDAwMDAx&ntb=1 == Overview == The {{ill|Honden|en|Honden|qid=Q2044994|lt=honden|1=Honden}} of the shrine is a wooden structure in {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}}, while the {{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|en|Haiden (Shinto)|qid=Q3125745|lt=haiden|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} is built in {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} and surrounded by {{ill|sacred fences|ja|瑞垣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred fences|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉垣}}. The grounds span approximately 920 {{ill|Pyeong|en|Pyeong|qid=Q706688|lt=tsubo|1=Pyeong}} (about 3,041 square meters). The main deity is Kazukime-no-Kami, identified with the legendary ama Oben. She is revered as the progenitor of ama divers, offering protection against dizziness and marine hazards. The shrine is also a pilgrimage site for divers across Japan. == History == In the 26th year of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}} (4 BCE), {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qid=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} visited Kuzaki while searching for offerings for the {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. Oben presented abalone, which impressed Yamatohime and was subsequently dedicated to Ise. This marked Kuzaki’s selection as one of Ise’s {{ill|Kanbe (social class)|lt=Kanbe (social class)|lt=Divine rice fields|ja|神戸 (民戸)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/divine rice fields|qq=}}. The shrine was renamed Ama Kazukime Shrine in 1869 and later consolidated 17 smaller shrines between 1906 and 1907. == Rituals == === Hachiman Festival === Originally the festival of Hachiman Shrine, it centers around archery rituals held on {{ill|January 5|en|January 5|qid=Q2202|lt=January 5|1=January 5}}. Key rituals include the "Manabashi" ceremony, where chosen men prepare fish with chopsticks and knives without touching it by hand. === Two Boat Festival === Held annually over three days in November, this festival involves competitive boat races between the Kuzaki subregions of Satodani and Kaimadani. Participants offer fish and {{ill|Omiki|en|Omiki|qid=Q3313722|lt=omiki|1=Omiki}} at the {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} during the race, believed to predict the year’s fishing fortune. === Mikazuki Ritual and Grand Festival=== Historically, this ritual involved female divers from seven villages gathering on the lunar calendar's June 1 to dive for abalone. Although discontinued in 1871, the ritual has been periodically revived for commemorative occasions, such as the 2016 G7 Ise-Shima Summit. * == Subsidiary Shrines Enshrined Together == The Amamochime Shrine enshrines the following 17 shrines, which were merged into it in 1906 and 1907. All of these shrines originally held no specific Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): pp. 980–982. {| class="wikitable" style="background-color:#fee0fb; width:40%" |- |style="color:#fff; background-color:#994C00" |'''Shrine name''' |style="color:#fff; background-color:#994C00" |'''deity Enshrined''' |style="color:#fff; background-color:#994C00" |'''Former location''' |style="color:#fff; background-color:#994C00" |'''Year of Merger''' |- |Tsurugi-no-Miya Shrine||{{ill|Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto|ja|天目一箇神|1=Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mahitotsu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}||rowspan="7"|Within Amamochime Shrine grounds||rowspan="14"|1906 (Meiji 39) |- |Shirahige Daimyojin Shrine||{{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} |- |Yakumo Shrine||{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |- |Kagami-no-Miya Shrine||{{ill|Ishikori-dome no Mikoto|en|Ishikori-dome no Mikoto|qid=Q6080112|lt=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto|1=Ishikori-dome no Mikoto}} |- |Yoroi-zaki Shrine||{{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} |- |Izawa-no-Miya Shrine||{{ill|Izawa-no-miya|lt=Tamahashira-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|玉柱屋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izawa-no-miya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊雑宮}} |- |Yama-no-Kami Shrine||{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563|lt=Oyamatsumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} |- |Hachiman Shrine||{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ojin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}||Miyanotani |- |Kazemori Shrine||{{ill|Shinatsuhiko|ja|級長津彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}||Kaima Valley |- |Tsuchi-no-Miya Shrine||{{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama|1=Ukanomitama}}
{{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|en|Kamuo Ichihime|qid=Q11588867|lt=Kamuo Ichihime|1=Kamuo Ichihime}}||rowspan="2"|Otsuzaka |- |Tsukiyomi-no-Miya Shrine||{{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|en|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q595520|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} |- |Marebito Shrine||{{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}||rowspan="2"|Within Tsukiyomi-no-Miya Shrine grounds |- |Taka-no-Kami Shrine||Tatehi-katano-Mikoto |- |Mizu-no-Kami Shrine||{{ill|Ame no Murakumo|ja|天村雲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Murakumo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天押雲根命}}||— |- |Shimmei Shrine||{{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}||Miyanotani||rowspan="3"|1907 (Meiji 40) |- |Kumano Shrine||{{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} |- |Yahashira Shrine||{{ill|Amaterasu and Susanoo's Covenant|ja|アマテラスとスサノオの誓約|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu and Susanoo's Covenant|1=Five Male and Three Female Deities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11284880}} |} === Tsurugi-no-Miya Shrine === Tsurugi-no-Miya Shrine (剱宮社) was originally located on Tsurugi Cape. It was merged into Amamochime Shrine in 1906 (Meiji 39). It is also known simply as Tsurugi Shrine. According to legend, one of the {{ill|Imperial Regalia of Japan|en|Imperial Regalia of Japan|qid=Q327465|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|1=Imperial Regalia of Japan}}, the {{ill|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|lt=Kusanagi Sword|ja|草薙剣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi no Tsurugi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天叢雲剣}}, which sank during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|en|Battle of Dan-no-ura|qid=Q968676|lt=Battle of Dan-no-ura|1=Battle of Dan-no-ura}} alongside Emperor Antoku, was retrieved from the sea by a monk named Ajari Enjo and subsequently enshrined there. == Festivals == Amamochime Shrine is home to two major festivals in Kunisaki Town: the Hachiman Festival and the Two Boats Festival.Shimamoto (1994): p. 163. === Hachiman Festival === Originally dedicated to the Hachiman Shrine, this festival centers around an archery ritual held on January 5th. Hachiman Festivals are widely celebrated in fishing villages across Toba City, emphasizing prayers for bountiful catches and maritime safety.Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): p. 809. The festival includes: * '''"Hatsuyori" (January 2nd):''' Preparatory meetings for the archery ritual. * '''"Daikon Kaki" (January 4th):''' Households prepare the offerings for the next day. During the festival, participants perform the {{ill|Manabashi Ritual|ja|真名箸神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manabashi Ritual|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in which fish is filleted into sashimi using chopsticks and knives without touching it by hand.Toba City History Compilation Office (1991): p. 809. === Two Boats Festival === The Two Boats Festival (二船祭) takes place over three days in November.Toba Tourism Association "[http://www.toba.gr.jp/200/post_523.html Two Boats Festival]" <[https://megalodon.jp/2013-0421-1742-58/www.toba.gr.jp/200/post_523.html Web Archive]> (accessed April 21, 2013). The highlight is a boat race between the Kaima and Satodani communities, representing prayers for abundant fish catches. == Surroundings == The shrine is located in Kunisaki Town, near the center of Toba City. Visitors can take the Kamome Bus from the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|qid=Q872023|lt=JR Sangu Line|1=Sangū Line}} Toba Station to the Kunisaki stop, followed by a 7-minute walk. Note that there is no dedicated parking space nearby. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|qid=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival|1=Gion Matsuri}} * {{ill|Ofuna Festival|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofuna Festival|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} * {{ill|Suicide by Drowning|ja|入水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suicide by Drowning|1=suicide by drowning|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11388929}} == External Links == * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Saku City Tourism Association - Iwamurada Gion Festival] * [https://web.archive.org/web/20160310135303/https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/bunkazaijimusyo/shiteibunkazai.html List of Designated Cultural Properties - Saku City] * [https://amakadukime-jinja.com/ Ama Kazukime Shrine official website] ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Suwa Faith|qq=}} {{Mikami Faith|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|海士潜女神社|version=102253313|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:海士潜女神社]] [[ja:岩村田祇園祭]] [[fr:Ama Kazukime Shrine]] [[zh:Ama Kazukime Shrine]] {{wikidata link|Q17191773}} [[Category:Son-sha in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:海女]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Toba, Mie]] [[Category:History of Toba, Mie]]
